evals.prompt: I'm planning to migrate my MySQL e-commerce database to Aurora DSQL in us-east-1. It has 10B rows across 50 tables with 150 indexes. We get about 50K read queries per second and 5K writes per second. Can you estimate the monthly cost?
evals.expected_output: Queries awspricing for us-east-1 DPU and Storage pricing, uses Default DPU Values, calculates monthly costs with breakdown, highlights largest cost driver, suggests optimizations
expectations: Calls awspricing search_products for us-east-1 DPU pricing (AuroraDSQL service)
Calls awspricing search_products for us-east-1 Storage pricing (AuroraDSQL service)
Uses Default DPU Values for estimation (0.063 for writes, 0.00047 for reads in writes, 0.003 for read compute)
Provides monthly cost breakdown showing Read DPUs, Write DPUs, Compute DPUs, and Storage costs separately
Identifies the largest cost driver (likely Read DPUs for this read-heavy workload)
Suggests at least one optimization strategy (e.g., add indexes, reduce rows scanned, batch writes)

evals.prompt: What would it cost to run a DSQL cluster with 100K write TPS and 1M read TPS in eu-west-1? I have about 50TB of data.
evals.expected_output: Queries awspricing for eu-west-1 pricing, calculates costs using Default DPU Values, provides cost breakdown, mentions regional pricing differences, suggests optimizations for high-TPS workload
expectations: Calls awspricing search_products for eu-west-1 region (not us-east-1)
Mentions that estimates use Default DPU Values or states assumptions clearly
Provides a total monthly cost estimate
Breaks down costs into Read DPUs, Write DPUs, Compute DPUs, and Storage
Mentions that EXPLAIN ANALYZE would provide more accurate estimates with real queries
Suggests cost optimization strategies relevant to high-TPS workloads

evals.prompt: I want to estimate DSQL costs but I don't have my cluster set up yet. I'm building a multi-tenant SaaS app in ap-northeast-1 with expected traffic of 10K reads/sec and 1K writes/sec. Data size will be around 5TB.
evals.expected_output: Follows the cost estimation workflow: asks about region (already provided), queries awspricing, uses Default DPU Values, provides estimate with assumptions clearly stated, offers to help design schema for better cost optimization
expectations: Calls awspricing search_products for ap-northeast-1 region
Explicitly states assumptions (e.g., 'This estimate assumes...' or mentions Default DPU Values)
Provides cost breakdown showing the percentage or amount for each component
Mentions that this is a rough estimate (+/- 30% or similar caveat)
Offers to help optimize schema or refine estimate with more details
Does NOT ask for region since ap-northeast-1 was already provided in the prompt
